title: Social media & texting language
description: Understand common digital communication styles â€” abbreviations, emoji use, and the difference between formal and informal digital writing.

content.title: Texting and social media language
content.body:
In digital communication, people often use SHORTENED LANGUAGE â€” especially in casual messages:

COMMON ABBREVIATIONS:
â€¢ btw = by the way
â€¢ lol = laughing out loud
â€¢ omg = oh my goodness
â€¢ tbh = to be honest
â€¢ imo = in my opinion
â€¢ fyi = for your information
â€¢ asap = as soon as possible
â€¢ brb = be right back
â€¢ ngl = not gonna lie
â€¢ icymi = in case you missed it

IMPORTANT: These are ONLY for casual, informal messages to friends. Never use them in formal emails, professional messages, or with seniors/strangers.
content.grammar_note:
Abbreviations = informal only
Capitalisation often dropped in texts
Emoji use = casual contexts only

content.title: Online etiquette â€” dos and don'ts
content.body:
GOOD digital communication:
âœ“ Reply promptly when possible
âœ“ Use full words in professional messages
âœ“ Double-check before sending
âœ“ Match the tone of the person you're writing to
âœ“ Use emoji sparingly and appropriately
âœ“ Be clear â€” avoid confusion

AVOID:
âœ— All caps (sounds like shouting)
âœ— Sending too many follow-up messages
âœ— Using abbreviations in professional contexts
âœ— Sharing personal/private information publicly
âœ— Responding emotionally when angry â€” wait before replying
âœ— Reading messages and not replying for days

A good rule: Would I say this to their face? If not, reconsider.
content.grammar_note:
ALL CAPS = shouting in digital communication
Professional messages: full words, no abbreviations
Read, pause, then reply if emotional

vocabulary.example: Check the email thread for the full conversation.

vocabulary.meaning: to mention someone in a post
vocabulary.example: She tagged me in her Instagram post.

vocabulary.meaning: a private message on social media
vocabulary.example: Send me a DM with the details.

vocabulary.meaning: suddenly stopping all contact without explanation
vocabulary.example: He just stopped replying â€” total ghosting.
What caption did she put under that photo?

sentences: Btw, are you coming to the meeting tonight?
I'll send you the details in a DM.
That video of the dog completely went viral!
Check the email thread â€” the answer is in there.
Omg â€” have you seen what she posted? That's hilarious!

content.question: Which message is appropriate to send to a NEW COLLEAGUE you've just met at work?
options: Omg ur so funny lol ðŸ˜‚ðŸ˜‚ðŸ˜‚
Hi Priya â€” great to meet you today! Looking forward to working together.
btw when r u free tmrw?
HEY PRIYA!! WHEN ARE YOU FREE????
content.correct: Hi Priya â€” great to meet you today! Looking forward to working together.
content.explanation: New colleague = semi-formal. Full words, friendly but professional tone, no abbreviations or all caps.

content.question: What does 'tbh' mean?
options: to be here
to be honest
talking by heart
to be helpful
content.explanation: TBH = to be honest. Often used before sharing a genuine or slightly uncomfortable opinion.

content.question: You want to send an update to your entire team about a meeting. Which message is most appropriate?
options: FYI meeting moved to 3 btw!! lol
TEAM MEETING IS NOW AT 3 PM!!!
Hi team â€” just a quick update: tomorrow's meeting has been moved to 3 PM. Please update your calendars.
Hey all â€” 3pm not 2pm tmrw k?
content.correct: Hi team â€” just a quick update: tomorrow's meeting has been moved to 3 PM. Please update your calendars.
content.explanation: Team communication = professional. Full words, clear information, no unnecessary abbreviations or all caps.

content.question: What is 'ghosting' in digital communication?
options: Posting scary Halloween content online
Suddenly stopping all contact without any explanation
Using a fake profile name
Replying very slowly
content.correct: Suddenly stopping all contact without any explanation
content.explanation: 'Ghosting' = disappearing from a conversation or relationship without explanation â€” considered very rude.

content.instruction: Expand these abbreviations into full words.
Rewrite this casual text message as a professional team message.

sentences.text: 'omg meeting tmrw is cancelled btw!! lol so happy' â†’ 'Hi team â€” ___'
sentences.answer: Hi team â€” just to let you know, tomorrow's meeting has been cancelled. Hope everyone has a good day!
Could you please send the report as soon as possible? Thank you.

content.instruction: Arrange into a professional team message.
content.answer: Hi team â€” a quick update: the meeting has been moved to 4 PM.

content.instruction: Arrange into a casual text to a friend.
content.answer: Btw, are you coming to the dinner tonight?

content.instruction: Match the digital communication issue to the correct advice.

pairs.left: Someone sends you an angry message
pairs.right: Wait before replying â€” don't respond emotionally.
Use full words and professional tone â€” no abbreviations.

pairs.left: You see a controversial post on social media
pairs.right: Think carefully before commenting â€” your digital footprint matters.

pairs.left: A friend hasn't replied for 3 days
pairs.right: They may be busy â€” don't send too many follow-ups.

pairs.left: You're in a group chat and want to share a sensitive update
pairs.right: Consider a private DM instead of the public group.

content.title: Group chat vs formal update â€” colleagues, Pune
content.setting: Sonal is part of two chats â€” a casual group with friends and a professional team group. Watch how her language changes completely.

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (friends chat)
dialogue.text: Omg you guys â€” the project is DONE! We can finally breathe! ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰

dialogue.speaker: Riya (friend)
dialogue.text: Yesssss!! Lol finally!! Are you coming out to celebrate tonight btw?

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (friends chat)
dialogue.text: Obviously!! Ngl I've been dreaming about this moment for weeks ðŸ˜‚

dialogue.speaker: Kavya (friend)
dialogue.text: Same omg. Btw where are we going? Imo that new rooftop place looks amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (friends chat)
dialogue.text: Yaas! I'll dm you all the address asap. Brb â€” need to send a proper update to my work team ðŸ˜…

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (WORK team chat)
dialogue.text: Hi team â€” I'm pleased to share that we've successfully completed the Horizon Project as of today. Thank you all for your dedication over the past three months.

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (WORK team chat)
dialogue.text: A detailed summary report will be shared by end of day. Please review and share any final comments by Friday. Well done, everyone!

dialogue.speaker: Manager Arjun (work chat)
dialogue.text: Excellent work, Sonal. Thank you for leading this so professionally. The report looks great.

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (back to friends chat)
dialogue.text: Okay I'm back! Let's gooooo ðŸŽ‰ Dinner at 8?

dialogue.speaker: Riya (friend)
dialogue.text: 8 is perfect!! Btw so proud of you Sonal â€” you worked so hard for this! ðŸ’ª

dialogue.speaker: Sonal (friends chat)
dialogue.text: Tbh I couldn't have done it without you guys cheering me on lol. See you at 8! ðŸ˜

comprehension_questions.question: How does Sonal's language CHANGE between the friends chat and work chat?
options: It doesn't change at all.
She uses abbreviations only in the work chat.
She uses casual abbreviations and emoji with friends, but formal full sentences with her work team.
She is ruder to her friends than her colleagues.
comprehension_questions.correct: She uses casual abbreviations and emoji with friends, but formal full sentences with her work team.

comprehension_questions.question: What does Sonal share in the work team message?
options: Dinner plans
That the project is complete and a report will follow
Her personal celebration plans
A complaint about the workload
comprehension_questions.correct: That the project is complete and a report will follow

comprehension_questions.question: What does 'ngl' mean in the friends chat?
options: next good lunch
not gonna lie
no good life
never give up laughing

discussion_questions: List 5 abbreviations or informal elements from the friends chat.
What makes the work message professional? List 3 features.
Why is it important to match your language to the audience?

content.instruction: These messages have register mistakes. Rewrite correctly.

sentences.wrong: Email to HR: 'omg can u send the form asap?? thx!!'
sentences.correct: Dear HR, Could you please send me the required form at your earliest convenience? Thank you.
sentences.explanation: Email to HR = formal. No abbreviations, full sentences, polite request.

sentences.wrong: Work announcement: 'THE MEETING IS AT 3 NOT 2 FYI!!'
sentences.correct: Hi team â€” just to confirm: today's meeting is at 3 PM, not 2 PM. Please update your calendars.
sentences.explanation: All caps = shouting. Professional messages need full words and a calm tone.

sentences.wrong: WhatsApp to a close friend: 'I am writing to enquire whether you are available for dinner this Saturday evening.'
sentences.correct: Hey! Are you free for dinner this Saturday? ðŸ˜Š
sentences.explanation: This is too formal for a close friend on WhatsApp â€” be natural and casual.

content.scenario: You see an online post that makes you very angry. You want to respond. What should you do?
options: Reply immediately with a very strong, emotional response.
Type your response in capital letters so they understand you're serious.
Wait until you feel calm. Think about whether replying will help the situation. If you respond, keep it respectful and factual.
Share it everywhere immediately.
content.correct: Wait until you feel calm. Think about whether replying will help the situation. If you respond, keep it respectful and factual.
content.explanation: Good online etiquette: never react when emotional. Pause, think, and respond with facts â€” not feelings.

content.instruction: Compare two types of messages â€” informal (to a friend) and formal (to a manager) â€” for the same situation: you need to take a day off tomorrow.
content.prompt: Write and say two messages â€” one casual to a friend, one professional to your manager â€” about taking a day off tomorrow.
content.example_response:
Here are both versions!

Casual WhatsApp to my friend Meera: 'Hey! Ngl I'm totally exhausted ðŸ˜… I'm taking tomorrow off! Btw can we do our walk on Saturday instead? Omg I cannot wait to just sleep in lol ðŸ˜‚'

Professional message to my manager: 'Hi [Manager's name] â€” I wanted to let you know that I'm not feeling well and would like to take a sick day tomorrow if that's okay. I'll make sure all urgent tasks are completed or handed over before I log off today. I'll share a brief update on pending work by 5 PM. Please let me know if this causes any issues. Thank you for understanding.'

You can see the difference clearly â€” with my friend I use abbreviations, emoji, and very casual phrasing. With my manager, I explain the situation clearly, offer to manage my work responsibly, and ask for permission politely. Same situation, completely different language â€” and that's exactly how it should be.
key_phrases: formal: I wanted to let you know, I'll ensure, Thank you for understanding

content.instruction: Think about how social media and digital communication have changed the way people communicate in India. What are the positive changes? What are the problems? Share your opinion.
content.prompt: Discuss the impact of social media and digital communication on everyday life in India â€” pros and cons.
content.example_response: Social media and digital communication have completely transformed how people communicate in India â€” and there are both good and not-so-good sides to this. On the positive side, staying connected with family members who live far away has become so easy. My cousins are spread across Chennai, Bangalore, Hyderabad, and even the UK â€” and we all talk on a family WhatsApp group every day. Without that, we'd barely know what was happening in each other's lives. Digital communication has also made business much faster â€” everything from job applications to client meetings can happen online now. I think that's genuinely wonderful. However, there are problems too. A lot of people â€” especially young people â€” spend far too much time on social media and it affects their mental health. They compare their lives to others and feel inadequate. There's also the problem of misinformation spreading very quickly through WhatsApp forwards. And tbh, sometimes I think people have forgotten how to have a proper face-to-face conversation â€” everyone's on their phone even when they're together. It's a bit sad. So overall â€” a brilliant tool, but we need to use it wisely.

content.instruction: Label each message as FORMAL (F) or INFORMAL (I) and correct any register mistakes.

sentences.text: 'Hi team! Wanted to flag that the deadline is tmrw â€” pls submit ASAP!!' [F or I? Correct if needed]
sentences.answer: Informal register in a professional context. Correct: 'Hi team â€” a reminder that the deadline is tomorrow. Please submit your work as soon as possible. Thank you.'

sentences.text: 'Omg I literally cannot come tonight â€” so sorry!!' [F or I?]
sentences.answer: Informal â€” appropriate for texting a friend. No correction needed.

sentences.text: 'I am writing to enquire if you received my application.' [F or I?]
sentences.answer: Formal â€” appropriate for an email to a company. No correction needed.

sentences.text: 'Hey â€” meeting at 3 btw ðŸ˜Š' (to your manager) [F or I? Correct?]
sentences.answer: Too informal for a manager. Correct: 'Hi [name] â€” just a quick note that the meeting is at 3 PM today.'

content.title: Lesson 6 complete â€” Social media and texting language
key_points: Abbreviations (btw, omg, lol, fyi) = informal ONLY â€” never in professional messages
ALL CAPS = shouting in digital communication â€” avoid in professional contexts
Match your register to your audience â€” casual for friends, professional for colleagues
Good online etiquette: wait before replying when emotional, don't over-message, use emoji appropriately
Same message, different register = completely different language
content.next_lesson: Online communication â€” professional chat and email
